name: Zeelandic
notes: STUB (deliberate): the grapheme inventory of Zeelandic is NOT specified. Zeêuws is a West Germanic (Low Franconian) variety of Zeeland and Goeree-Overflakkee. A written norm DOES exist — the Zeêuwse Schriefwiezer (Stichting Zuudwest 7, 1999) — but it defines its signs relationally against Standard Dutch and contains NO IPA whatsoever: <ae> is 'a sound closest to AN [aa] but more closed', <ao> 'closest to AN [aa] but more open', <eê/ê>, <oô/ô>, <aè>, <ò>, <è> likewise. It is explicitly PAN-DIALECTAL by design — a spelling in which all Zeelandic (and West/French Flemish) dialects can be written 'without compromising their own character' — so a grapheme such as <ae> deliberately does not denote one IPA value. No source consulted (en/nl Wikipedia, Encyclopedie van Zeeland, uusvantzeeuws.nl, Omniglot — whose chart is an image) gives a letter->IPA table. Documented features (monophthongal [i] and [y] retained for <ij> and <ui>, umlauted [aː] -> [ɛː], h-dropping, r = [r~ɾ~ʁ]) are not enough for an honest map. A defensible spec must first pick ONE variety (e.g. Walcheren) and cite a dialectological source for its vowel values. INPUT CONTRACT (for any future spec): Zeelandic text in the Zeêuwse Schriefwiezer spelling.

sources.author: Stichting Zuudwest 7
sources.title: Zeêuwse Schriefwiezer
sources.notes: The Zeelandic spelling norm. Defines its signs by comparison with Standard Dutch and gives no IPA; deliberately pan-dialectal, so its graphemes do not map to single IPA values.

ancestors.notes: Germanic — the classification node this language sits under.
